title: ABS Signal & Idle Up Relay Circuit Check

figures_detected.description: Circuit diagram showing IGN-SW, A/C relay, Idle-up relay, N-CVT C/U, A/C clutch, Solenoid, and ABS C/U connections with fuse ratings 10A #2 and 10A #21
Diagram A: N-CVT C/U connector showing terminal #25 with voltmeter connection to ground
figures_detected.associated_step: Check idle-up relay - Check input signal step 4

figures_detected.description: Diagram B: Idle up relay connector showing terminal #1 with voltmeter connection to body ground
figures_detected.associated_step: Check idle-up relay - Check idle-up relay power circuit step 4

content_blocks.text: ABS Signal & Idle Up Relay Circuit Check

content_blocks.title: ABS Signal & Idle Up Relay Circuit Check

nodes.text:
Check ABS signal circuit
• Perform 'ABS' self diagnosis using CONSULT. Does 'CVT TRANSMISSION WIRE' indicate a faulty circuit?
• If 'ABS' self diagnosis is performed, does lamp blink ten times?
(Refer to section BR, 'ABS TROUBLE DIAGNOSIS' for self diagnosis method)
Repair harness or connector between N-CVT control unit terminal #14 and ABS control unit terminal #32

Check idle-up relay
Check input signal
1. Turn ignition switch 'OFF'
2. Disconnect N-CVT control unit connector
3. Turn ignition switch 'ON'
4. Check voltage between N-CVT control unit vehicle harness connector terminal #25 and ground.
Voltage: Battery voltage

Check idle-up relay
Check idle-up relay power circuit
1. Turn ignition switch 'OFF'
2. Remove idle-up relay
3. Turn ignition switch 'ON'
4. Check voltage between idle up relay connector terminal #1 and body ground
Voltage: Battery voltage
Check harness or connector between fuse and relay

model_notes: Applies to N-CVT (CVT transmission) equipped vehicles with ABS. References N-CVT C/U terminal #14 and #25, ABS C/U terminal #32.
